observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to consistently recognize the purchased inventories to their respective asset accounts and practice proper derecognition thereof; and (b) MGSO and MAO to ensure coordination between their offices for the reconciliation and timely recording of receipts and issuances of inventories.
observations.reason: (Reiterated this AAR 2024)

observations.recommendation: We reiterated, with modification, our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer to (a) prepare the IIRUP to include all unserviceable properties to facilitate the proper disposal of the unserviceable properties; and (b) create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee which will undertake the necessary actions for the immediate disposal of the said properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that a Disposal and Appraisal Committee has already been established and will be responsible for handling the disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to investigate the variance noted between the RPT/SET Receivables accounts and the RPT delinquency report and make necessary adjustments, if necessary, to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that due to the lack of personnel and training for existing staff, the Treasury Office has not conducted examinations of the books of accounts. However, the Treasury Office has committed to making efforts to conduct these examinations within this year.
observations.reason: The Treasury Office cited lack of personnel and training for existing staff as reason for non-implementation; committed to conduct examinations within the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Executive Chief instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the General Ledgers to update the book balances and to continually monitor the accounts to avoid mistakes and reflect the correct amounts of withholding and remittances made to the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG, thereby providing accurate information to readers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a schedule of the actual amounts withheld versus the remitted amount for BIR Taxes, HDMF, PhilHealth and GSIS Contributions and commented that any amount found in the SL to be not in agreement with the actual amounts will be adjusted accordingly.
observations.reason: (Reiterated this AAR 2024)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
(a) direct the City Assessor to consider requesting for additional manpower and to oversee the completion of tax mapping activities that will enhance the cleansing and improvement of the City’s real property assessment and tax collection;
(b) require the City Assessor to prepare and submit to the City Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year an assessment roll that includes a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed, along with the values of those properties. This will serve as the basis for the City Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers; and
(c) thereafter, require the City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant, at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the taxpayers' names and the amount due and collectible for the year. The City Accountant will use this list as a basis for preparing a JEV to set up the RPT and SET Receivables, along with the corresponding Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income.
observations.managementAction: The City has initiated efforts to complete its tax mapping. The City Treasurer’s Office stated that preparing the certified list of taxpayers relies on the records of the City Assessor’s Office. Consequently, they will wait for the said Office to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Assessment roll not yet submitted to the City Treasurer; tax mapping and correction/updating of the city's real property database remain incomplete; limited manpower in the Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the OIC - City Accountant to investigate the discrepancies in the beginning balances of the Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG, and Due to PhilHealth accounts, and make appropriate correcting entries based on the results of the investigation.
Furthermore, we recommended that the OIC - City Accountant and the City Human Resource Management Officer continue their efforts to analyze, trace, and determine the details of any unreconciled or unremitted amounts due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth, and to effect adjustments and/or immediate remittances to the corresponding agencies, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction:
The Management commented that some of the discrepancies were due to timing differences in recording. It also stated that the P15,400.00 Pag-IBIG Contributions erroneously withheld from 77 JO workers are already being processed for refund to the workers.
The Management also assured that the recommendation to continue their efforts in analyzing, tracing, and determining the details of the unreconciled or unremitted amounts due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth will be followed and observed.
observations.reason: Discrepancies due to timing differences; beginning balances unreconciled since transition from manual to automated recording; further tracing/reconciliation required.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to jointly evaluate the impairment of the receivables and to comply with the documentary requirements and guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to facilitate the submission to COA of the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables amounting to P1,756,156.60 or to avail of the one-time cleansing of dormant receivables within one year from the effectivity of COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management. The request for write-off the dormant receivables amounting to P1,756,156.60 is yet to be resubmitted by the Agency for evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Request for write-off not yet resubmitted to COA; documentary requirements for evaluation not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor, through the Head of the HRDMO, expedite the hiring and/or promotion of qualified personnel to reduce if not totally eliminate the need to appoint casual and JO workers, thus ensuring a more efficient and effective government service thru strategic management of human resources.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2024, the Management has adjusted/ corrected the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) under the CY 2024 LDRRM Plan to include only programs for four thematic areas of disaster risk management as set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1. The Management also indicated actions on hiring and noted 1,600 hired JO workers.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2024, there are still 369 unfilled Plantilla positions and hired JO workers totaled 1,600; ongoing hiring/regularization efforts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to submit the distribution lists duly signed by the actual recipients/beneficiaries and pictures showing actual distribution for validation of actual distribution of rice, supplies and materials to the affected residents due to Typhoon Egay and fire incident in Barangay Talaba.
observations.managementAction: The Office has coordinated with the City Social Welfare and Development (CSWD) for the list of recipients and pictures for the list pertaining to the distribution of rice and other supplies. Copies were not yet provided to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Copies of distribution lists and supporting pictures not yet provided to the Audit Team; validation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by strictly following the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had yet to draft the Physical Inventory Plan, which will serve as the basis for conducting the one-time PPE cleansing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management review the Municipality’s functions, systems and procedures, organizational structure, and staffing pattern to address any needs and gaps in the human resource complement.

We also recommended that Management comply with all the provisions of the JC and consider existing qualified COS/JO workers when filling all its vacant plantilla positions, and provide for wages of COs/JOs at a rate equivalent to a comparable position in the government plus premium if funds are available.
observations.managementAction: Management will update its plantilla positions taking into consideration the functions, systems, and procedures, organizational structure, and staffing pattern aligning to the Municipality’s PPAs. After this, management will conduct an evaluation and assessment of all COS and JOs to identify who are best suited to fill the vacant plantilla positions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management endeavor to implement the projects during the budget year and become proactive in utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs that will eliminate flood risks, lessen the adverse impacts of hazards and calamities, or prepare and train residents on how to appropriately respond to any disasters that may happen.

Reiterated under Finding No. 9, Part II of this report.
observations.managementAction: Management had not maximized the use of the LDRRMF since most of the national government projects allocated to the Municipality had been focused on mitigating the risks of hazards and calamities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.managementAction: The LRNLC had been maintained by the Accounting Office and total road networks booked had already been disclosed in the Notes to FS.
observations.reason: Some accounting and reporting guidelines still needed to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the BAC to judiciously validate the eligibility of the suppliers in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and stop the practice of procuring supplies from a supplier whose goods or services that are the subject of procurement are not included in the “line of business” of the supplier’s BIR CoR and Mayor’s Permit.

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management undertake their procurements through Competitive Bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR.
observations.reason: Other suppliers transacted with the Municipal Agriculturist Secretariat still need to be verified.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management undertake their procurements through Competitive Bidding in accordance with Section 10, Rule IV of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of the IRR.

We also recommended that Management observe the judicious application of Shopping as the alternative mode of procurement when the conditions provided under Section 52.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 are established.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of additional honoraria to the Barangay Health Workers (BHWs) and Barangay Nutrition Scholars (BNS) pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 63, Section 95 of R.A. No. 7160 and Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution to avoid incurring illegal expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, through the Mayor, entered into a Memorandum of Agreement with the BHWs and BNSs to pay a monthly honorarium for the services rendered. The component barangays of the Municipality had transferred their budget allocation for the payment of honoraria of the BHWs and BNSs to the Municipality.
observations.reason: The recommendation was for the opposite, for the Municipality to provide funds to the Barangay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned lacking documents as required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 to enable us to properly evaluate the propriety of the disbursements.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a certification that there are no arrearages in Real Property Taxes on the acquired properties as of the date of purchase.

We furthermore recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before payment is made in compliance with Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Time constraints and the tedious process caused delays in the titling since the Municipality has to depend on other Agencies in order to proceed.

Management had submitted a certification from the municipal treasurer in CY 2021 that two of the three lots mentioned have no real property tax arrearages as of the date of purchase, but not for the third lot.
observations.reason: Some transactions still had some lacking documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the depository account maintained with the RBBI to either Land Bank of the Philippines, Dumaguete Branch or Development Bank of the Philippines, Dumaguete Branch, in compliance with Section 311 of RA No. 7160 and DOF Department Circular No. 01-2017 dated May 11, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had resorted to maintain their deposits with the RBBI for convenience and to help the local economy of the Municipality. The Municipality had already closed the account it maintained with PVB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure systematic procedures while strictly adhering to existing guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.
observations.reason: The issue with the previous disbursements had yet to be resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) on all donations received by the Municipality and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Certification submitted only included a list of LSIs but lacked details on the goods received duly acknowledged by the recipients. Also, there was no inventory for the undistributed goods, if any.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management appropriate/realign ₱876,500.00 in the 20% Development Fund and ₱66,800.00 in the General Fund Proper and reimburse the LDRRMF for the cost of the catering services, printing of home quarantine/ECQ passes and cremation of one PUI.

We further recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DBM LBC No. 124 dated March 26, 2020 on the use of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted documents to support the responses to COVID-19; however verification still needs to be done.
observations.reason: There is still a need to check the year-end balances and to fully verify the transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management prepare the following reversal entry:

Account Title Debit Credit
Land 11,677,081.19
Government Equity 11,677,081.19

We also recommended that Management exhaust all means to determine the acquisition costs of Lot Nos. 1840, 1841, 1851-B, 1852, 1855, 1856, 1894, and 3181 so that appropriate adjustment in the recorded costs may be made accordingly.

Lastly, we recommended that, henceforth, Management adhere to the provisions set forth under PPSAS No. 17 for the accounting treatment for property, plant, and equipment to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE maintained that the appraisal should be taken up as part of the Notes to Financial Statements (FS) only to conform to the basic accounting principles of materiality and conservatism.

Management had been verifying the acquisition costs to effect the proper adjustments.
observations.reason: The determination of acquisition costs is still ongoing and there were accounting adjustments to be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts were submitted but not within the reglementary period. The documents submitted were not certified true copies.
observations.reason: The copies submitted were not certified true copies. Also, the certification of the MBO, Accountant and Treasurer duly noted by the LCE, and pertinent Promissory Note and other collateral documents had not yet been submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already transferred the payment of the honoraria of the BHWs and BNSs to the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer had requested the issuance of AF No. 51 from the leagues.
observations.reason: The VMLP still had not issued AF No. 51.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Council include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF. We further recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRM on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Reports had been prepared by the Municipality but not submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to record all lots owned by the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count thereof and reconcile the same with related records maintained by the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management was still in the process of drafting the Physical Inventory Plan, which shall serve as the basis for the one-time cleansing of PPE. The Municipal Accountant will record the necessary accounting entries once the one-time cleansing is completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the members of the BAC/TWG allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. We further recommended that the BAC/TWG should conduct post-qualification in accordance with Section 34, Rule X of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Transactions for the year still need to be evaluated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality conduct competitive bidding in the procurement of cattle in accordance with Section 10 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained and more farmer beneficiaries are benefited.

We also recommended that the farmer beneficiaries be required to return the excess grant of capital assistance.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that it was not possible to procure the cattle through public bidding because there was no qualified supplier in the province; thus the method of releases was converted to giving cash to farmer beneficiaries for them to personally buy cattle on their own. The beneficiaries did not fail to pay their obligation so a next batch of beneficiaries could avail of the loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality close its deposit accounts with the PVB and RBB and deposit the same to a government financial institution granting high interest rates.
observations.managementAction: Management opted to maintain their deposits with RBBI for convenience and to help the local economy of the Municipality. The Municipality had already closed the account it maintained with PVB. However, its RBBI account was still being maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure their common-use supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Management had resorted to Shopping and may consider procuring from the depot if it is no longer impractical due to distance, cost, and time constraints. The LGU had noted that the supplies at PS-DBM depot were of inferior quality or had been in stock for a long time due to physical appearance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observed Section 52.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods.
observations.reason: Procurements for the year still need to be evaluated after year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant see to it that 10% warranty security is deducted from the amount claimed to protect the interest of the LGU pursuant to 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Procurements for the year still need to be evaluated after year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer require the taxpayers to submit an Income Statement/Statement of Receipts and Expenditures when applying for renewal of business taxes to ascertain the accuracy of the declared gross receipts subject to tax. If the examination of the books could not be conducted immediately, the taxpayers may be required to submit their preceding year’s financial statements as temporary bases in computing the business taxes.
observations.managementAction: Examinations of business taxpayers had been done for larger establishments, but manpower constraints prevented full coverage of all taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended:
a. The Mayor to strictly require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and for the municipal treasurer’s office to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations.
b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property Plant and Equipment to Other Asset account and to look into the disbursement vouchers and other documents pertaining to the unbooked assets and record the same in the book of accounts.
c. The Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing hand held radio issued to the former barangay captain of Barangay Calangag and the Iwata Time Recorder amounting to ₱24,642.50 and ₱14,950.00, respectively. Also, locate the engine previously installed in the LGU owned patrol boat and get back the 70CC, Econo, power Honda kept in a private residence and henceforth, refrain from storing government properties in a private residence/place.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had still been in the process of drafting the Physical Inventory Plan, which shall serve as the basis for the one-time cleansing of PPE. The reconciliation shall be done once the one-time PPE cleansing is completed.
observations.reason: The items had yet to be located.

observations.recommendation:
If the employees concerned still fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, we recommended that Management instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries.

Henceforth, require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Completed projects had not yet been transferred from trust funds. The accountable officer with an unliquidated cash advance was no longer in service due to a transfer.
observations.reason: The accountable officer with overdue cash advances did not have their salary withheld but the cash advances remained unliquidated; transfers and time gaps hampered enforcement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets.
b. Settle the Due to NGAs account by submitting Liquidation Reports to the grantors so that the latter’s books are also adjusted accordingly, before effecting the transfer to the General Fund.
c. Based on the physical inventory, require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS.
d. Require the LGU to maintain and regularly update a properly accomplished Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor.
observations.managementAction: The projects had not yet been funded out of trust funds and the PPELCs had not been updated due to lack of manpower. Management planned to conduct a physical inventory once the plan is completed.
observations.reason: Projects had not been transferred out of trust funds despite completion; the conduct of physical inventory still needs to be evaluated at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. Members of the Bids and Awards Committee/ Technical Working Group should conduct thorough evaluation and review eligibility requirements for the Procurement of Goods and Infrastructure Project.
b. The BAC allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services.
c. In the post qualification, the BAC/TWG should verify, validate, and ascertain the genuineness, validity and accuracy of the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB before awarding the same.
d. Personnel preparing the purchase orders should strictly indicate the basic data on the PO pursuant to Section B.1 of COA Circular 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
e. The agency should submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 138 (f) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume 1.
observations.reason: Transactions for the year still need to be evaluated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of the monthly trial balances and financial statements within the reglementary period in accordance with Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, otherwise, we shall be constrained to withhold the salary of concerned officials and implement other sanctions and penalties as provided for under our laws.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is gearing towards digitalization and is aiming to be able to generate financial reports within the reglementary period by then.
observations.reason: Reports were not submitted within the reglementary period due to delays in processing source documents and transition to digital systems.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to assign specific personnel to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements to COA and the depository bank so that timely verification and review can be made and discrepancies immediately identified and corrected.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already prepared the monthly bank reconciliation statements; however, these had not been submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Consider increasing rental rates or implement measures to reduce operating costs of the municipality’s economic enterprises, and require the municipal treasurer to accelerate the collection of unpaid rentals by sending bills of collection to delinquent stall or space lessees, invoking the sanctions provided under the Contract of Lease to make the collection more effective. In addition, look for additional revenue sources, such as charging minimal fees for the use of the market’s comfort rooms.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already drafted its revised revenue code subject for review and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Conduct a Physical Inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets. b. Settle the Due to NGAs account by submitting Liquidation Reports to the grantors so that the latter’s books are also adjusted accordingly, before effecting the transfer to the General Fund. c. Based on the physical inventory, require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS. d. Require the LGU to maintain and regularly update a properly accomplished Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had stopped recording the purchase of the projects as outright expense and started using RIS and SSMI. The PPELCs had not yet been updated due to manpower limitations.
observations.reason: Physical inventory still needed to be conducted and reconciliations completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Members of the Bids and Awards Committee/ Technical Working Group should conduct thorough evaluation and review eligibility requirements for the Procurement of Goods and Infrastructure Project. b. The BAC allow only eligible prospective bidders to participate in the public bidding for the procurement of goods and services. c. In the post qualification, the BAC/TWG should verify, validate, and ascertain the genuineness, validity and accuracy of the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by the bidder with the LCB before awarding the same. d. Personnel preparing the purchase orders should strictly indicate the basic data on the PO pursuant to Section B.1 of COA Circular 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. e. The agency should submit the lacking documents pursuant to Section 138 (f) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume 1.
observations.reason: Future procurements still have to be continuously validated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of the monthly trial balances and financial statements within the reglementary period in accordance with Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, otherwise, we shall be constrained to withhold the salary of concerned officials and implement other sanctions and penalties as provided for under our laws.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is moving towards digitalization and aims to be able to generate financial reports within the reglementary period.
observations.reason: The Municipality was still transitioning to digital systems and had not been able to submit within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Accountant to assign specific personnel to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements to COA and the depository bank so that timely verification and review can be made and discrepancies immediately identified and corrected.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had prepared the monthly bank reconciliation statements; however, these had not been submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Consider increasing rental rates or implement measures to reduce operating costs of the municipality’s economic enterprises, and require the municipal treasurer to accelerate the collection of unpaid rentals by sending bills of collection to delinquent stall or space lessees, invoking the sanctions provided under the Contract of Lease to make the collection more effective. In addition, look for additional revenue sources, such as charging minimal fees for the use of the market’s comfort rooms.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already drafted its revised revenue code subject for review and approval.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. Consequently, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive remind
the Municipal Accountant to comply with the timely submissions of the
BRSs at the beginning months of the year.

2.14 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider the creation
of a GSO plantilla position to appoint a separate GSO officer so that
proper reporting and monitoring of Inventories could properly be
conducted.
2.15 We further recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and the
Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (a) constantly coordinate
with the MT for the existing inventories under their possession, and (b)
properly coordinate with the MT regarding the distribution of the
inventory items under their possession to appropriately report for the
movements of the inventories at a point in time.
2.16 Moreover, we recommended that the MT (a) prepare the SC, SSMI, AIR
and RPCI, as provided under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1, Section
44, Chapter 2, Volume 2 and Annex 31 and 35 of the MNGAS for LGUs
and submit the same to this Office in adherence to the said guidelines; (b)
conduct a periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above
regulation, and (c) reconcile the records of the MTO with the Municipal
Accountant.
2.17 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) coordinate
with the MT for the reconciliation of the Inventory account; (b) prepare
necessary correcting journal entries, if any, to present fairly the
Inventory account in the FSs; (c) ensure that all donated inventories are
taken up in the proper Inventory sub-account to reflect the proper
amount of the Inventories, and (d) adhere with the provision under
IPSAS 1 with regards to the faithful representation of the transactions in
the FSs.

3.16 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) consider the
creation of a GSO Plantilla position to appoint a separate GSO officer so
that proper reporting and monitoring of the PPEs could be properly
conducted; (b) provide a PIP and submit it to the COA to formally apply
for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts as per COA Circular No.
2020-006; (c) address the discrepancies between the unreconciled
General Ledger balances and the Inventory Report on current year's
conduct of physical inventory, and (d) submit the WMR in accordance
with the provisions stated under Section 59 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
3.17 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make necessary
adjustments on the CIP account to reflect the correct balance of the PPE
account as of CY 2024.
3.18 Further, we recommended that the MT maintain PC for each class of
PPE as stated in Annex 37 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
3.19 Lastly, we recommended that the MT and the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate for the reconciliation of the PPE records.

4.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) adjust the
erroneous entry made during the awarding of the contract to reflect the
correct entry as of December 31, 2024, and (b) review pertinent rules and
regulations pertaining to Construction in Progress so as not to incur any
additional erroneous entries.
4.11. We as well recommended that the Municipal Engineer regularly
communicate with the contractors for any progress billings
corresponding to percentage of completion of the project.

5.23. Consequently, we recommended that Municipal Accountant: (a) exert

observations.reason: The Management also asserted that due to manpower constraints, the one-time cleansing will not be possible to be completed within this year, hence, discrepancies will still be present on the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board: (a) enforce the proper wastes segregation at source to reap the benefits of the “No Segregation No Collection Policy” instead of having mixed waste materials left strewn in public places; (b) ensure that wastes in the Market Transfer Station are timely collected and transported to the Municipal MRF and observe cleanliness in its surrounding; (c) provide each barangay with the needed garbage truck, subject to availability of funds, and facilitate the establishment of MRFs in barangay-level in accordance with its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan for the period of 2022-2031.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Officer direct the (a) MDRRMC to include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous year in the LDRRMFIP; and (b) Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002. We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive Officer direct the MDRRMC to (i) monitor the implementation of the LDRRMP to optimize the utilization of its LDRRMF in order to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, including projected climate risks, (ii) submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU as mandated in COA Circular 2012-002. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) record purchase of welfare/relief goods and other expendable items to inventory account and issuances thereof to the appropriate expense accounts; and (ii) maintain corresponding Subsidiary Ledgers for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment set forth under COA Circular 2014-002 and COA Circular 2012-002, respectively; and (iii) to record the ₱2,000,000.00 FA received in a separate SL of the Trust Liability account and utilize the said NDRRMF in accordance with the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that the Accounting Office has already begun creating a subsidiary ledger (SL) for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment. Additionally, the recording of the ₱2,000,000.00 financial assistance received has been reclassified to the Trust Liability account, with a separate SL established in the Special Trust Fund. The MDRRMC has also begun actions to monitor implementation of the LDRRMP.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our Auditor's recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Accountant and the various Departmental Heads to coordinate and strictly observe the timely preparation and submission of the Financial Reports, DVs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the reglementary period prescribed under the aforementioned Circulars; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to assess if additional workforce would be necessary to facilitate the prompt submission of the documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that due to the overload of functions and responsibilities on the employees in the Accounting Office, additional manpower is necessary; thus, the Municipality should consider hiring additional staff to facilitate the prompt submission of documents to the Audit Office.
The City Implemented the remaining variances is still ongoing. Reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-City Accountant to:
(a) Finalize the ongoing verification to determine the cause of the noted discrepancy, and make necessary correcting journal entries on the erroneous recording of transaction based on the result thereof; and
(b) Exercise caution in classifying and recording transactions in order to avoid such deficiency in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Management has identified some of the discrepancies and provided the correcting journal entries, which were drawn only on March 05, 2025, under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) Nos. 100-2025-03-GJ0001, 0002, 0003, 0004, 0005, 0006 and 0007. The remaining balance of P650,202.76 is subject to further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
(a) Direct the Accountable Officers (AOs) to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
(b) Require the OIC-City Accountant to investigate the long-outstanding unliquidated cash advances described as ‘Over/Under Collection' and take appropriate actions based on the results of the investigation; and
(c) Instruct the OIC-City Accountant to refrain from posting journal entries for liquidations by batch and at the end of each month, instead record the same on the day the liquidation, together with complete supporting documents, were submitted by the AO to the City Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided the corresponding liquidations only in CY 2025 for the mentioned outstanding cash advances. The Management also stated that for the “Over/Under Collection,” the investigation and determination of appropriate actions are ongoing. They further committed to posting journal entries on the actual date of the liquidation and not at the end of each month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC - City Accountant and the City Treasurer to facilitate the return of the unexpended fund transfers totaling P18,463,365.72 to the source agency or National Treasury or to proceed with the immediate utilization of the non-moving funds for their intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Management declared that some of the programs or projects have ongoing activities in CY 2025 for the purpose of utilizing the intended funds; hence, the Management encouraged the beneficiaries/end users to act quickly to make use of the funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
(a) Direct the OIC-City Engineer to coordinate with the contractors to post the necessary public notices and signboards of the six on-going projects in compliance with the mentioned regulation, otherwise a notice of disallowance will be issued; and
(b) Ensure that all PPAs carried out by the City comply with public notification requirements regarding full disclosure of all transactions involving public interest.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided a letter reminding the contractors to post the necessary public notices/signboard and committed that the City will comply with the public notification of all information on these PPAs for full disclosure of all transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
(a) Require the OIC-City Engineer and the OIC-City Accountant to make representations to Meralco for the resolution of the electric bill to permit the withholding of taxes as required by regulation;
(b) Instruct the OIC-City Engineer to ensure that the contractors file complete documents with Meralco to qualify for the deduction of withholding tax on electricity consumption for newly constructed buildings before the turnover to the City; and henceforth,
(c) Instruct the OIC-City Accountant to ensure that proper taxes are withheld before the payment of transactions and to subsequently remit the same to the BIR, in accordance with RMO No. 23-2014 and RR Nos. 11-2018 and 13-2018.
observations.managementAction: The Management is committed to undertaking the necessary activities and submitting the documentary requirements to Meralco so that they can be properly tagged as a withholding agent, ensuring that billings reflect the amount of tax withheld.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC - City Accountant to:
(a) Exert extra effort to trace and clarify the details of the beginning balances of Due to BIR and ensure their immediate remittance and/or make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts as the case may be; and
(b) Strictly comply with BIR RMO No. 23-2014 and BIR RR Nos. 11-2018 to ensure that the correct amount of taxes is withheld and properly remitted within the prescribed period to prevent unnecessary surcharges and penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented that P5,985,821.94, out of the aggregate amount, was remitted to the BIR in February and March 2025. The remaining balance is still subject to further investigation and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management close its checking account with LBP and return the remaining balance of the Trust Fund to the DILG to avoid incurring future dormancy fees on the dormant bank account.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of R.A. No. 7305 and revised IRR for the grant of hazard pay to its PHWs.

We further recommended that Management secure the documentary requirements for the payment of hazard pay to the PHWs as listed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate an additional DO to avoid granting multiple cash advances to the incumbent DO.

We also recommended that no additional cash advances be granted to the DO unless the previous cash advance is settled or properly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: Per verification of records, the incumbent DO was still able to draw more than one cash advance a day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances granted to the DO Designate do not exceed the maximum cash accountabilities covered by his fidelity bond, otherwise, effect the increase of his fidelity bond commensurate with his accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019, to protect the interests of the Municipal government.

We recommended that the HRMO-Designate ensure that the accuracy, authenticity, and completeness of all the requirements and supporting papers in connection with all cases of appointments are thoroughly reviewed and checked before submitting the same to the CSC to avoid disapproval/invalidation of appointments.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Planning and Development Council to include in the Annual Investment Plan and eventually in the Municipality’s proposed budget only those programs and projects that are “Implementation-Ready”.

We also recommended that Management address the problem of the funded projects that have become unimplementable due to changes in events or conditions such as price changes or site conditions, among others. Management should review its projects funded under the continuing appropriation over the last three years or any timeframe as they deem appropriate, to ascertain if the projects are still viable for implementation at this time. Otherwise, consider their reversion, in accordance with Section 322 of RA No. 7160, to release funds and make them available for other meaningful uses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had yet to draft the Physical Inventory Plan, which will serve as the basis for conducting the one-time PPE cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up an accounting entry in the General Fund (GF) and the Trust Fund (TF) books to record the obligation of the unexpended QRF and the authorized receipt of funds from the GF, respectively, for the transfer of the unexpended balance of the QRF at the end of the year using the accounts discussed in the foregoing paragraphs in compliance with the accounting requirements under Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA) ensure that loan adjustments such as repricing be effected in the proper accounting period to reflect the correct balance of the loans payable.

We also recommended that the MA conduct periodic analysis of the Loans Payable-domestic account and coordinate with LBP and MDFO to reconcile and update records maintained by the parties. Discrepancies noted should be reconciled, and the necessary adjustments be effected to ensure a fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management take immediate action to comply with the requirements stated in DOH AO No. 2018-001 dated January 28, 2018 in order to obtain the required license for the ambulance. This will enable the Municipality to rectify the existing non-compliance and ensure that the ambulance operates in accordance with the necessary standards and regulations established by DOH.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before payment is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management had already started recording the subsequent payments as Other Receivables.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DBM LBC No. 124 dated March 26, 2020, on the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM motor vehicles be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021. Otherwise, refund or replenish the LDRRMF for the cost of the subject motor vehicles and heavy equipment, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management noted that the LDRRM motor vehicles were used only when necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO and the Property Officer require the drivers of the LDRRM vehicles to accomplish properly the prescribed form for the Driver’s Trip Tickets to ensure that all the data needed to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed for the period and the necessity of the trips undertaken are provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the amount granted as seed capital fund to the farmer’s associations recorded to expense or liability accounts to the proper asset account “Other Receivables” (1-03-06-990) by making the following adjusting entry:

Other Receivables ₱9,550,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱100,000.00
Trust Liabilities ₱4,300,000.00
Trust Liabilities – DRRMF ₱2,100,000.00
Due to NGAs ₱3,050,000.00

We further recommended that Management make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts for the seed capital assistance granted as a loan to the beneficiaries of the backyard cattle fattening project in CY 2020 and prior years when the Municipality started to appropriate funds for the said project from its own Internal Revenue Allotment.
observations.managementAction: Management had started recording the subsequent payments as Other Receivables.
observations.reason: The Municipality had not completed all the documents needed for verification to support the adjustments to be made in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms by using the RIS to request and issue supplies and materials carried in stock and to consolidate the same in the SSMI, which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording to the appropriate expense accounts for the supplies consumed.

Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to expense accounts and henceforth, to record purchases and utilization of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section IV of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had stopped recording the purchases as outright expense and started using RIS and SSMI.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that before any repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment are undertaken, Management require the personnel in charge to prepare the following reports, namely: a) pre-repair evaluation report and approved detailed plans showing in sufficient detail the scope of work/extent of repair to be done, b) report of waste materials, c) document indicating the history of repair, and d) post-inspection report.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursements for the repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and heavy equipment are supported with the required documents enumerated under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: There was still a lack of requirements for the supporting disbursement vouchers covering payments for repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles with the Pre-inspection Report, Waste Materials Report; and Post-inspection Report but still lacked the History of Repair.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor copy of the Mayor’s/Business Permit, original OSS of the suppliers, duly notarized, using the form prescribed under RA No. 11469 and RA No. 11494, and PE certification stating that it exerted all efforts to secure the most advantageous price to the government.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted the OSS of New Bian Yek Commercial, ACME Traders, and Payalin Trading, Inc. only.
observations.reason: Similar transactions during the year still need to be examined to determine compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.reason: Purchases of medicines for the year still need to be evaluated to see if the medicines are purchased at the most reasonable price.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC require the suppliers to submit the original notarized OSS before or after award of contract but before payment is made and include a warranty clause in the contract or POs for procurements through emergency purchase in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted the OSS of New Bian Yek Commercial, ACME Corporation, and Payalin Trading, Inc. only.
observations.reason: Similar transactions during the year still need to be examined to determine compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a. Submit the following:
i. An explanation for proceeding with the canvass on the basis of a purchase request which lacked details and specifications and with bids which were unusually too similar, and despite the lack of details;
ii. An explanation as to why the caterers who participated in the canvass were able to come up with their bids despite the lack of a menu;
iii. A breakdown of each caterer’s menu offering; and
iv. A corrected Acceptance and Inspection Report specifying the items inspected.

b. Ensure that the procedures for Shopping under Section 52.1(a) of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 are satisfactorily adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Management had already submitted the rectified PUMs and Inspection and Acceptance Report.
observations.reason: Transactions for the year still need to be examined to determine compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the items above the highest suggested retail price of the DOH.

We further recommended that Management procure medical supplies based on the SRP set by appropriate government agencies to ensure that the Municipality get the most advantageous prices.
observations.reason: Purchases of medicines for the year still need to be evaluated to see if the medicines are purchased at the most reasonable price.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit copy of the approved contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.
observations.managementAction: The approved contracts of personnel under the COS and JO scheme still had to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We also recommended that Management submit the following documents or a written explanation why the same could not be submitted:

a. Relief distribution sheets for the relief and welfare goods showing the names of the beneficiaries with their signatures, kind and quantity of items received by each beneficiary, and the date of receipt;
b. List of frontliners and persons under monitoring who were catered with meals during ECQ and GCQ, duly signed by them; and
c. List of passengers who availed of the free transport service during the implementation of ECQ, duly signed by them.
observations.managementAction: Management had submitted some of the documents.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Submit the loan agreement together with the certified true copy of the SB Resolution bearing the signatures of all concurring SB members, BSP Opinion of the Monetary Board together with the requisite documents, BLGF-DOF certifications, certification by the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer noted by the LCE, the pertinent Promissory Note and other collateral documents, and the supplemental AIP; and

b. Renegotiate on a fixed interest rate, with primordial consideration of the Municipality’s capability to repay the loan within the incumbency of the officials’ 3-year term in office.
observations.managementAction: Renegotiation on the imposition of quarterly repricing of interest rates is currently underway. Management had also requested LBP to reconstruct the loans availed of by the LGU. Payments made by the LGU to the LBP in 2023 were marked “Under Protest” pending resolution of the matters.
observations.reason: Copies of documents had yet to be submitted within the reglementary period; some documents had been submitted but not certified true copies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the aforementioned lacking documents as required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 to enable us to properly evaluate the propriety of the claim.
observations.managementAction: The Capital Gains Tax and Estate Tax for lots 5126 and 1515 had already been paid.
observations.reason: The required documents had yet to be submitted to the office.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had maintained the Taxpayer’s Index Card but was not able to furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the year. The LGU is moving toward digitalization, which will ease the reconciliation of the total collectibles from the Basic and Special Education Tax with the balances of the Receivables. The Subsidiary Ledgers will also be maintained for each barangay.
observations.reason: Reconciliation had been stalled due to voluminous work in the offices concerned and unavailability of some documents.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that Management immediately submit to the Auditor all common and additional documentary requirements for the purchase of lots enumerated under Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Documents related to the lots had yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM projects are implemented and that Management train and equip volunteers as well as residents to be able to respond to any disaster or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The actions could not be validated as the documents needed for validation were not submitted.
observations.reason: The utilization for the year still needs to be evaluated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish the GAD database storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: The data for the GAD Database had been collected. Management had appropriated funds for the Community-Based Monitoring System but had not yet started implementing the project. The turnover of gathered data began in June 2025.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The actions could not be validated as the required documents needed for validation were not submitted.
observations.reason: The documents needed for validation were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame. We further recommended that the municipal ordinance on solid waste management be strictly enforced and all barangays be involved in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had established and operated a sanitary landfill due to limited financial resources. The Municipality had entered into an agreement with the City of Bayawan for the disposal of residual waste effective April 1, 2021. Management imposed upon residents of the Municipality to compost their bio-degradable wastes. Only residual wastes were collected and deposited in the dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize the filing of application for titling of the recorded properties to hasten the issuance of certificates of title in favor of the Municipality. We further recommended the Municipal Treasurer maintain property cards and the Municipal Accountant to update their records and reconcile with the Office of the Municipal Assessor.
observations.managementAction: The title for Lot No. 1581 had already been awarded to the Municipality. Updating and reconciling of records was still ongoing. The titling of other lots was not completed due to time constraints as the Municipality had to rely on other government agencies to proceed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Solid Waste Material Board immediately revise the Solid Waste Management Plan following the Annotated Outlined prescribed by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Commission for approval pursuant to Section 16 of RA No. 9003 for an effective solid waste collection and disposal.
observations.managementAction: The amended SWM had already been submitted to the NSWMC for approval. The NSWMC returned it for some corrections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1) Immediately send demand letters to the concerned accountable officer/other agency officials/employees; 2) If the accountable persons failed to liquidate, withhold their salaries; and 3) Require strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Completed. The municipal treasurer had started coordination to reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMC to: (a) prepare and submit a supplemental/revised LDRRMFIP to be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and the LCE for all new projects to be funded from the DRRM unexpended balances and include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous year and include projects to be funded by the supplemental budget in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002; and (b) strictly use the 30% Quick Response Fund (QRF) in accordance with Section 22 of RA 10121 and Section 6.3 of the DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC assured that new projects to be funded from previous years’ unexpended balances will be properly incorporated in a revised LDRRMFIP to be submitted to the LCE and Sangguniang Bayan for approval. Moreover, the implementation of all LDRRM PPAs as well as the evaluation of its effectiveness and sustainability is strictly being monitored by the MDRRMC.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Inventory Team (i) conduct complete annual physical inventory-taking of PPE; and (ii) submit a complete RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year; and (b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the other department heads, exert joint efforts to create a complete set of property records, property cards and ledger cards for each type of PPE and, henceforth, ensure that they are complete and regularly updated.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive designate a dedicated property officer, if the budget allows, to ensure that all functions pertaining to supply and property management are carried out properly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Inventory Committee was created to undertake the task of conducting a complete annual physical inventory-taking of PPE. To ensure maintenance of complete property records a General Services Section shall be created within the Municipal Not Implemented Treasurer’s Office Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation: We reiterated and Management agreed that the Management (a) exhaust possible means to secure copies of the TCT of the lots and buildings owned by the Municipality and submit to the Audit Team for verification; and (b) designate a personnel to work on documentation for the legality of ownership of the lots together with the structures built thereon; and (c) appropriate additional funds to cover the cost of transfer of titles.
observations.managementAction:
The Management agreed to adhere to the audit recommendations. The Management shall verify the status of the lots and take the necessary steps in acquiring copies of the TCTs.

The Municipal Inventory Committee shall make recommendations in coordination with the Municipal Legal Officer in documenting the legality of the said lots.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer to (a) prepare the IIRUP to include all unserviceable properties as basis in recording the de-recognition thereof from the books of accounts; and (b) facilitate immediate disposal of the said unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere to the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately avail the use of the ATM payroll system in the payment of wages for the Municipal employees, including the Casual and Job Order workers. This will protect not only the payroll funds from losses but also the lives of the Accountable Officer/s who do withdrawals of huge amount of cash from the bank. Likewise, the use of such system will efficiently reduce the hours spent in manually paying the wages of personnel.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere to the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer (a) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Projects Status Accomplishment Report of all infrastructure projects, on-going and those that are to be implemented during the year, to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation thereof; (b) strictly comply with the full disclosure policy of the government by putting up the appropriate project signboards and notices on all the municipality’s on-going projects and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award and before the start of the program/activity to validate the same for transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere to the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) if possible, limit essential and regular duties of JOs in core offices to permanent and casual employees, particularly those with accountability for government funds; (b) require all department heads to carefully evaluate the performance of JOs and consider those who perform well for regular positions; (c) require the JOs assigned at the legislative department to use the biometrics for purposes of fairness; and (d) submit accomplishment report on the job/tasks done in support of the payroll.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to adhere to the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Accountant and the various Department Heads to coordinate and strictly observe the timely preparation and submission of the Financial Reports, DVs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the reglementary period prescribed under the aforementioned Circulars; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to assess if additional workforce would be necessary to facilitate the prompt submission of the documents to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The concerned departments/ officials will submit the necessary lacking documents in the procurement of the sampled infrastructure projects and goods, and to all projects to be implemented and goods to be procured this year onwards.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for the proper withholding and full remittance of the monthly taxes and GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG contributions in accordance with the requirements set forth under Revenue Regulations 1-2013, Republic Act 8291, PhilHealth Circulars 0001, s. 2014 and 0009, s. 2019 and HDMF Circular No. 274, respectively.

However, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Executive Chief instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the General Ledger to update the book balances and carefully monitor the accounts to reflect the correct amounts of withholding and remittances made to the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG, thereby providing accurate information to readers.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office thanked the Audit Team for their commendation. The Accounting Office also commented that the tracing of said balances is now going to prepare the necessary adjustments and update the book balances. In addition, the accounting personnel in charge of withholding and remittances were instructed to strictly monitor the accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this CY 2023 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate with each other to evaluate the impairment of the receivables and to complete the necessary documents and comply with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to facilitate the submission to the COA of the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivables amounting to P1,756,156.60 or to avail of the one-time cleansing of dormant receivables within one year from the effectivity of COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Management. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5, Pages Nos. 50-52.
observations.reason: The Management was not able to request for write-off of dormant receivables.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer and other concerned officials to prepare and submit the necessary requirements to enable the City to include in its automated payroll system the payment of the wages of its Job Order workers.
observations.managementAction: The Management has included the wages of several Job Order Workers in the automated payroll system.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to promptly update, balance, and certify the collections, deposits, and disbursements on the cashbooks, ensure the correctness and accuracy of entries recorded therein, and henceforth adhere to the existing regulations on the handling, custody, and disposition of the cashbooks.
2. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors to ensure the fairness of the presentation of the cash and cash equivalent account balances in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and the Municipal Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.managementAction:
No physical count of the total road networks was conducted in CY 2023 as the LGU cannot ascertain the total road networks within the Municipality due to the lack of turnover of projects undertaken by the DPWH and Province.
The LRNLC had already been maintained by the Accounting Office and total road networks booked had already been disclosed in the Notes to FS. However, some accounting and reporting guidelines still needed to be implemented.

observations.managementAction: Management had already reclassified the vehicle as a patient transport vehicle instead of an Ambulance. No. 7160 and Article IX-B of the 1987 Philippine Constitution to avoid incurring illegal expenditures.

If the Municipality wishes to continue the granting of additional honoraria to the BHWs and BNSs, the funds therefor shall be given to the component barangays as aid, pursuant to Section 324(c) of RA No. 7160.
