observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the liquidation report to COA and Bureau of Soils and Water Management and refunded the balance thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: The Municipal Budget Officer provide justification why the procurement of utility ambulance for use of the Rural Health Unit was charged to 20% Development Fund; The BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant submit the proof of posting from the PHILGEPS where the Invitation To Bid was advertised; the official receipt on payment of the bid documents; tax clearance; minutes of bid opening; proof of posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in PHILGEPS; acknowledgement receipt for equipment (ARE); and the certified true copy of the Official Receipt (OR)/Certificate of Registration (CR) in the name of the Municipality; The end-user presents the utility ambulance to the audit team to establish its physical existence.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer submitted the copy of justification on the reason why procurement of utility ambulance was charged to 20% Development Fund. The BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and other concerned personnel are continuously exerting all efforts to provide compliance to COA requirements on the said submission to avoid disallowance in audit. The end-user presented the utility ambulance to the audit team to establish its physical existence. Management provided justification letter dated September 1, 2017 of the Municipal Accountant on the non-imposition of warranty security in the year 2014.
observations.reason: For evaluation upon submission of the documents as required in the Notice of Suspension. Still in the process of reproducing and reconstruction of other supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman submit the paid disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents for the purchase of road grader and mini dump truck, rescue boat, and generator totaling P4,056,591.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted documents for the purchase of rescue boat and generator. The Municipal Accountant is in the process of reconstructing other lacking documents of road grader and mini dump truck to complete those which were previously submitted before to COA before typhoon Nona occurred and caused devastation.
observations.reason: For evaluation upon submission of the documents as required in the Notice of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management cause the immediate submission of all paid DVs with their corresponding supporting documents and require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the above regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Treasury personnel submitted all recovered intact and damaged paid DVs and its supporting documents.
observations.reason: For evaluation and subject of request for relief from accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (a) reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due From Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, (b) review and analyze the causes of discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, and (c) ensure that all accounting entries are consistent with prevailing rules and regulations and to generally accepted accounting principles.
observations.managementAction: In the AAPSI, adjustments were made and reflected in the 2016 financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: The Local Chief Executive requires the concerned municipal officials and employees to refund the payment of P1,579,200.00 representing RATA differential or the P1,080,760.00 representing payment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive which was made in excess of the 45% PS limitation, and ensure that the appropriation and expenditures for Personal Services are within the 45% PS limitation as mandated by Section 325(a) of RA 7160. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer require the refund the excess payment of RATA to the Municipal Health Officer in the amount of P1,500.00, and ensure compliance with the provisions of DBM LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 in the payment of RATA; and The Municipal Accountant instruct the staff in-charge in the preparation of payroll of Representation and Transportation Allowances (RATA) to consider the actual work performance or the approved leave applications of the concerned officials and employees vis-à-vis their respective RATA.
observations.managementAction: Partial refund of the excess amount of PS was made during the year through deduction in the bonuses of the employees. The Municipal Accountant continues to deduct the refundable amount to all officers and employees.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still deducting the refundable amount to all officers and employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: (a) Honorable Mayor and concerned officials revisit the regulation on the collection of water fees in order to formally intensify the collection aspect, adopt realistic strategies to improve its collection efficiency and acquire additional water meter to monitor the actual water usage of every barangay connected to the water system; (b) the Municipal Treasurer assign collectors to enforce the collection, and send notices to all households reminding them of their obligation to pay water consumptions as well as the penalty for late payment, and coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Engineer in the furnishing of the list of delinquent water users; and (c) the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer to impose disconnection of line for failure to pay the water fee within two months.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has approved the Revised Revenue Code. The Municipal Treasurer assign collectors to enforce the collection aspect and acquire additional water meter to monitor the actual water usage of every barangay connected to the water system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer require the contractor the immediate settlement/ correction of the above cited deficiency to recover the loss on the part of the government due these underruns in project implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality informed that the matter was the subject of an Appeal Memorandum filed to COA Region IV. The management previously attached the copies of the letter relative to the matter. The management, despite no knowledge of what really transpired during the past administration where deficiency occurred, awaits for the final and executory decision of the commission for full authority in the implementation of collection of payment from the concern accountable supplier or persons.
observations.reason: An Order dated September 9, 2011 was issued by the Director, COA Regional Office No. IV directing the Appellees to submit an Answer to the Appeal together with a certified copy of the entire records of the case including the Excerpts of Documents and Summary of Events (EDSE) every page of which shall be numbered at the bottom within fifteen (15) days from receipt pursuant to Sections 18.1 and 18.2, Chapter IV of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) and Section 6, Rule V of the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the COA (RRPC).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer make representation with the DBP-
Calapan Branch regarding the reconciling item of P2,425,000.00 and
take immediate corrective action to reflect the accurate bank account
balance;
b. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant conduct
further analysis of the bank reconciling items reflected in the
submitted BRS to ascertain its validity, and make necessary
adjustments; and
c. The Municipal Accountant (i) verify the status or whereabouts of the
stale checks which were reflected as outstanding checks in the BRS and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the affected
accounts; (ii) effect necessary adjustments for the unrecorded interests
which remained unadjusted in the books of accounts to reflect the
correct balance of Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account; and
(iii) coordinate with the concerned servicing banks for the timely
submission of the bank statements in order to prepare and submit the
BRS to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period in
accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We reiterated our prior-year recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant adopt the imprest system for the petty cash transactions and adhere
to the prescribed procedures for the establishment, liquidation, and
replenishment of the PCF pursuant to Section 48 of the Manual on the NGAS,
Volume I. Moreover, discontinue the practice of charging financial aid in the
PCF.

We recommended that the:
a. concerned officials and employees who attended the abovementioned
trainings to refund the excess travel claims to avoid disallowance in
audit; and
b. Municipal Mayor requires the officials and employees that all travel
claims are reviewed by the immediate supervisors to ensure that travel
is necessary and reasonable and consistent with the existing rules and
regulations.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that:
a. The Municipal Inventory Team (i) conduct complete inventory of all its
PPE and prepare the RPCPPE according to type of PPE in accordance
with Section 57 and Annex 49 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume
II, respectively; and (ii) conduct complete inventory of all unserviceable
properties and prepare the required IIRUP so that disposal could be
effected pursuant to applicable laws, rules and regulations on the
supply and property management; and
b. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer continue the preparation and
maintenance of property cards for PPE acquired from CY 2017 and
earlier pursuant to Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Recover/collate all the necessary documents supporting the liquidation
of the fund transfer from the Department of Agrarian Reform, and
prepare the required entries to reflect the effect of the same in the
appropriate affected accounts;
b. Review the liquidation of fund transfers with completed projects to
facilitate the return/refund of unutilized balances to the respective
source agency; and
c. Ensure preparation of the supporting SLs/schedules for each purpose of
fund transfer for each source agency for proper monitoring and
reconciliation purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the
proper account classification in recording transactions and adjust the Other
Payable and other affected accounts for its fair presentation in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Secretariat provide proof of compliance of the posting of the ITB
at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality pursuant
to Section 21.2.1 (a) and (b) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
submit all the lacking documents for audit purposes;
b. Municipal Engineer submit to the Audit Team the Drawings/Plans duly
approved by the Municipal Mayor for the project Improvement of
Municipal Cemetery; and the documents supporting the 100%
accomplishment and completion of the project Partial Completion of
Wet Market; and
c. Municipal Accountant ensure that all contracts are duly documented to
support the propriety of payments to the contractor.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Secretariat (i) provide proof of compliance of the posting of the
ITB at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality
pursuant to Section 21.2.1 (a) and (b) of the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184; and (ii) post all NOA, and NTP and the approved Contract in
the PhilGEPS in accordance with Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the
IRR of RA No. 9184;
b. BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant (i) submit all the lacking
documents for audit purposes; and
c. Municipal Accountant review the accounting entries made to record
the transactions and effect necessary entries to adjust the affected
accounts.

We recommended that the:
a. Management render a satisfactory explanation on the following:
i. payment of consultancy contract with AUB Consult Management
and Engineering Services for the Preparation of Detailed
Engineering Design amounting to P845,000.00 without the DILG
approval of the Technical Design or DED;
ii. failure to take out and maintain insurance to cover against the risk
of fire, flood and other damages to the facilities constructed as
required in Section 2.1 (a) and (b) of the MOA;
iii. delay of 180 and 176 days before the Notice of Award (NOA) and
Notice to Proceed (NTP), respectively, were posted in PhilGEPS;
iv. inconsistencies and other observations in various supporting
documents;
v. non-adherence in the Short Listing of Prospective Bidders under
Section 24.5 of the IRR of RA No. 9184; and
vi. failure to consider the criteria on the technical proposal of the
winning bidder and using the corresponding numerical weights
indicated in the Bidding Documents as per Section 33.2.2 of the
Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
b. Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
reflect the correct balances of the Advances to Contractors,
Construction-in-Progress, and Other Payables accounts as of December
31, 2017; and (ii) deduct from the amount due to Karangyan Builders
and Traders the cost deficiency of P93,199.11 or demand payment for
such amount, as the case may be;
c. Municipal Engineer monitor and institute corrective measures on the
findings of the COA TAS; and
d. Municipal Engineer, BAC Chairman, and Municipal Accountant
submit the lacking documents to support the procurement activities in
the contract for the Improvement and Expansion of PAG-ASA Water
System Phase I and Consultancy contract in the preparation of Detailed
Engineering Design for the project to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BAC Secretariat
submit all the lacking documents for audit purposes;
b. Municipal Agriculturist provide satisfactory explanation in the
inconsistencies on the process of request, order, receipt, issuance and
payment of procured materials relative to Organic Fertilizer
Production; and
c. Municipal Accountant (a) provide explanation on the procured items
which remain unpaid to date, (b) compute and impose the liquidated
damages for the supply and delivery of construction materials for the
Shallow Tube Installation, and (c) ensure that all disbursement
vouchers are fully supported with complete documentations before
payments to the suppliers.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal School Board (i) strictly comply with Item 5 of DepEd,
DBM and DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017, inviting
attention to Section 5.5 of the circular which provides the guidelines
and schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF
budget, and (ii) consolidate all requests for procurement, prepare and
submit the PPMP for supplies and materials, and prepare a
consolidated APP, copy furnished the Audit Team;
b. Municipal Treasurer submit the liquidation report of the
Municipality’s support for the 2017 Provincial Meet; and
c. Municipal Accountant record the acquisition of the construction
materials and other inventory items first to its proper inventory
account and to proper expense account upon its issuance.

We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation that the SWM Board
adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open/controlled dumpsite
and to provide the necessary funds for its proper operation, and prioritize the
implementation of solid waste projects in order to prevent health, sanitation and
environmental hazards. Further, the MENRO-Designate follow-up other
barangays of the Municipality regarding their SWM Plan to facilitate its
consolidation.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant:
a. Prepare an adjustment to record the earned income from market
operation not yet recognized at year end;
b. Record as debit to Operating Lease Receivable (1-03-02-010), and credit
Receipts from Market Operations (4-02-02-150) and Fines and
Penalties-Business Income (4-02-02-980) accounts the accrued revenue
arsing from market operation pursuant to the Revised Chart of
Accounts under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;
and
c. Analyze the Market Stall / Lot Occupants Record to recognize
additional revenue accruing to the Municipality from stall owners with
unpaid rental fees and penalty.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) analyze the
unremitted balance of Due to GSIS totaling ₱48,226.19 and effect the necessary
adjustments and corrections, if any; (ii) analyze the individual accounts of the
monthly billings and remittances to GSIS by reconciling the over or under
remittance of loan amortizations, and effect adjustments and corrections, if any;
and (iii) strictly impose the deduction and remittance of loan amortizations such
as consolidated loans, policy loans, emergency loan, housing loan and other loans,
and other amounts due to GSIS.

We commended the Offices of the Municipal Accounting and Treasury for
their continuous adherence to revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the
taxes due to the BIR. Further, we however, recommended that the Municipal
Accountant perform analysis of the Due to BIR account so that any discrepancy
may be resolved at earliest opportunity.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents relative to the liquidation of the P4,848,800.00 fund transfer to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant explain why it took more than four years before the liquidation report is submitted to this Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit promptly and regularly all paid payrolls as well as approved daily time records (DTRs), approved application for leave, and other pertinent supporting documents pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree 1445 and Section 5.10.1 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, as reiterated in Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Personnel Officer to ensure that supporting documents for liquidation of personal services are provided.

observations.managementAction: No action taken by management

observations.managementAction: No action taken by management

observations.recommendation:
2.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a) Municipal Inventory Team conduct complete inventory of all its property,
plant, and equipment, and prepare the RPCPPE according to type of PPE in
accordance with Section 57 and Annex 49 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume II; and
b) Municipal Accountant and Inventory Team (i) reconcile the balances of the
PPE per books with the inventory reports, (ii) verify and analyze the
discrepancies, and (iii) prepare adjustments to the affected records.

3.4 We recommended and the Municipal Inventory Team agreed to conduct
complete inventory of all unserviceable properties and prepare the required IIRUP
so that disposal could be effected pursuant to applicable laws, rules and regulations
on the supply and property management.

4.11 We recommended and the Honorable Mayor agreed to enjoin the
department heads to assist the Municipal Accountant in the implementation of COA
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 for Local Road Network and COA
Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 in the implementation of the
accounting system and the new chart of accounts relative to other Public
Infrastructure accounts, inviting attention to the following responsibilities:
a. The Municipal Treasurer (i) maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map, (ii) keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components, (iii) render a Report on Local Road Network of
the Municipality at the end of the accounting period, and (iv) provide
copies of memorandum of agreements, service contracts and other
agreements with respect to deposits of funds and other contracts which
affect the accounting of financial assets and liabilities and the management
of physical assets of the local government unit; and
b. The Municipal Inventory Team (i) conduct an inventory of the local roads
under the Municipal Government’s jurisdiction, (ii) prepare the Report on
the Physical Count of the Road Network System, and (iii) reconcile the list
of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those
recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure;
c. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects identified, and provide report on the
cost and status of on-going projects and submit detailed cost of
components of infrastructure projects;
d. The Municipal Accountant (i) analyze the Registry of Public
Infrastructure on Roads and other Public Infrastructure accounts, and
identify the accounts to be recorded back in the books of accounts,
(ii) prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of
the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the
Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively, (iii) support the JV with
the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the
distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the
determination of the depreciated replacement cost for the road
components with no available cost per registry, (iv) keep and maintain
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road
network, and (v) prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each component at the end of the year; and
e. The Municipal Budget Officer provide the updated Registries of
Appropriation, Allotment and Obligations, and furnish copies of the
approved budget, supplemental budgets, realignment and distribution of
the lump sum appropriation and the corresponding legislative approval.

5.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adopt the
imprest system for the petty cash transactions and adhere to the prescribed
procedures for the establishment, liquidation, and replenishment of the PCF
pursuant to Section 48 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I. Moreover,
discontinue the practice of charging financial aid and other operational expenses
that are considered regular and recurring in nature in the PCF and require the
45
Disbursing Officer to have another cash advance specifically for one purpose like
financial assistance/aid to indigent.

6.5 We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer review and verify
the reconciling items identified in the BRS totaling P9,790.96 and effect
the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of
the Cash in Bank Account;
b. The Municipal Treasurer to take appropriate action regarding the Trust
Fund DOH bank account that incurred bank service charge for failure to
maintain the minimum balance requirement to prevent further reduction
of the cash balance.

7.7 We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer provide column headings in the record book and
additional column to record the date reported the accountable forms was
totally used in accordance with Section 95, Book II, Volume I of the
GAAM.
b. Collectors in the market issue cash tickets half-torn, such that one-half to
be given to the space occupant or vendor and retain the other half and
attach the same to the report of collections for proper monitoring
pursuant to Section 74, Book II, Volume I of the GAAM and Section
6C.04, Chapter VI of the 2015 Municipal Revenue Code; and
c. All collectors prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms
(RAAF) in its prescribed format for consolidation of the Municipal
Treasurer, in accordance with Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGU,
Volume II; and file requisitions of accountable forms for their use through
a Requisition and Issue Voucher and avoid the transfer of accountable
forms between collectors pursuant to Section 25 of COA Circular No. 92-
382, and ensure to indicate the correct beginning balances, issuances and
ending balance of accountable forms in the Report of Collection and
Deposit.

8.3 We recommended that the:
a. Management prioritize and implement the programs, projects and activities
identified during the year to ensure resilience of the community and capacity
to recover and respond from the impacts of disasters and emergencies; and
b. MDRRMO (i) prepare and submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan in
accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-
002 to reflect the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and
preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be funded, as well as
the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of
previous years; and (ii) prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal
Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in
accordance with the format provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-
002.

11.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to faithfully comply with the
aforecited provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and other
prevailing government procurement rules and regulations; and that all contracts
are duly supported with necessary documents prior to payment to the winning
contractor/s.
11.9 We also recommended that the:
a. BAC Chairman (i) post all NOA, and NTP and the approved Contract in
the PhilGEPS in accordance with Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the IRR of
RA 9184; (ii) indicate the date of the acknowledgement of receipt by the
contractor in the PO and completion date of the project in the Completion
Report; and
b. BAC Chairman and the Municipal Accountant (i) submit the aforecited
lacking documents and (ii) provide explanation why warranty security was
not imposed pursuant to Section 62 of IRR of RA 918.

12.5 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The BAC and the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the
lacking documents for audit purposes;
b. The BAC (i) provide satisfactory explanation on the non-posting of the
NOA, NTP, and approved Contract from its issuance, and Invitation to
Bid (ITB) in the PhilGEPS; and warranty security; and we recommended
that:
c. The BAC and the Municipal Accountant submit to the Audit Team the
lacking documents for audit purposes;
d. The BAC (i) provide satisfactory explanation on the non-posting of the
NOA, NTP, and approved Contract from its issuance, and Invitation to
Bid (ITB) in the PhilGEPS; and warranty security; and (ii) indicate the
acknowledgement date by the supplier in the NOA and NTP, and date in

e. The Management see to it that the Municipality (i) abides by the pertinent

contracts are duly documented to support the propriety of payments to the
contractor/s. indicate the acknowledgement date by the supplier in the
NOA and NTP, and date in the performance security and other bid
documents; and
f. The Management see to it that the Municipality (i) abides by the pertinent
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; and (ii) ensure that all
contracts are duly documented to support the propriety of payments to the
contractor/s.

15.5 We reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed that
the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and
analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects
for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section
322 of RA 7160 and those amounts reflected in the subsidiary ledger as
beginning balance or non-moving for years; and
b. Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects undertaken
by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and
Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances
of continuing appropriations, and submit a list of all completed projects to
support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated
surplus.

16.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to faithfully abide
with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised IRR in all the
procurement of the Municipality; and ensure that all contracts are duly
documented to support the propriety of payment to the contractor/s.
16.7 We also recommended that:
a. The BAC Chairman (i) post all NOA, and NTP and the approved Contract
in the PhilGEPS in accordance with Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the IRR of
RA 9184; (ii) ensure to indicate the date of the acknowledgement of receipt
by the contractor of the NOA and NTP, and the date and place of delivery
in the PO; and (iii) submit the lacking documents; and
b. The Municipal Accountant submit the Certification of Fund Availability.

18.6 We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Local
School Board prepare and approve the annual School Board Budget for SEF setting
forth the intended programs, projects and activities in accordance with the afore-
cited laws and regulations; and (b) discontinue the practice of utilizing the fund for
payment of MOOE of the DepEd Office and other expenses not related to or
included in the list of priority expenses chargeable against SEF.

19.5 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to prepare and submit
the monthly monitoring report of the awarded infra PPAs, as well as the list of on-
going PPAs, and those that are to be implemented during the year in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that:

The Municipal Mayor create and direct the Inventory Team to conduct the inventory of all property and equipment under the General and Special Education Funds, and submit the Inventory Report to the Municipal Accountant and to the Office of the Auditor. The designated property custodians may initially conduct the physical count of property and equipment in every office to facilitate the complete inventory of all PPE of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Accountant and ICO-Treasurer maintain ledger and stock or property cards, respectively, and undertake the reconciliation of the result of the physical count of PPE with their respective records for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements. The Municipal Accountant may draw a list of each department’s newly acquired property and equipment covering CY 2015 to date to start the maintenance of PPE ledger cards for each type of PPE, and work back and trace the available records for PPEs acquired in prior years. Further, the ICO-Treasurer may begin to maintain property records based on the deliveries of supplies or property for CY 2015 to date on the basis of the AIR and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order on the assignment of Property Custodian of each department to monitor and record procurement of PPE of their respective offices. 

The ICO-Municipal Treasurer conducted a new physical inventory taking starting from the new procurement and backwards to reconcile the correctness of treasury and accounting records on PPE.


The ICO-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant established PPE ledger cards for newly acquired PPE and those  PPE not damaged or  retrieved after Typhoon Nona and continues to maintain PPE ledger cards, conduct physical inventory on the existing PPE.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of the Report

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation and Management agreed that the Accounting and Treasury personnel exert extra efforts to locate the documents relative to the acquisition cost and date, and compute the depreciation of property identified with specific acquisition date and cost.
